methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Liminal Insights is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Barry Van Tassell and Shaurjo Biswas. Having raised $38.9M to date, it sits within a deep comparables universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, anchored by stage and sector matches including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two valuation methods triangulate the company's fair value. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $39M total raised proxy, implying $78M. The peer-median method draws on three same-stage, same-sector companies with valuations of $110M, $150M, and $200M respectively, yielding a median of $150M. The peer range spans $110M to $200M.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $66M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $114M. The dispersion between methods reflects material uncertainty around whether Liminal should command mid-market or upper-market positioning within the climate-tech Series A cohort. Stage-progression favors the lower end; peer medians support the upper-middle. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, technology differentiation, and path to Series B capital availability in the sector. A valuation in the $100M–$150M band appears most defensible given the evidence.
